stories.title: How to watch Romania’s political chaos like a pro
stories.summary: Romania is undergoing a fresh bout of political turmoil that could have far-reaching consequences for the EU’s sixth-most populous country.  Almost one year on from a divisive presidential election , defeated far-right candidate George Simion is now surging ahead in the polls and causing mayhem inside Romania’s parliament.  He is pushing a vote of no confidence in the government next week, which could topple Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan and trigger a period of instability that would thr

stories.title: EU plans ‘pre-entry’ perks for Ukraine as fast-track membership hopes dim
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU countries are preparing a package of short-term benefits for Ukraine to bring it closer to the bloc after capitals rejected plans to fast-track full membership. The emerging offer would include greater market access and deeper participation in EU programs and institutions, four diplomats familiar with the discussions, granted anonymity to discuss the confidential negotiations, told POLITICO. “Ukraine’s priority remains full EU membership,” Ukraine’s ambassador to t

stories.title: Trump says US considering reducing troops in Germany
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says the Pentagon is looking at withdrawing some of the nearly 40,000 American military personnel in Germany just days after the country’s leader sharply criticized the U.S.-led war in Iran. Trump said in a social media post late Wednesday that the U.S. is “studying and reviewing” cutting back the American deployment of troops to Germany that has for decades served as a prominent component of NATO’s defense of Europe and has strong support on Capitol Hill. Pentagon officia

stories.title: The missing Ukrainian reporter, the Russian prison – and a vital lesson learned about journalism in a dangerous age | Laurent Richard
stories.summary: To do our job, investigative journalists are learning to work across borders. This isn’t just about the truth, it’s about staying alive Laurent Richard is a journalist and the director of the Forbidden Stories consortium In February 2025, the body of journalist Viktoriia Roshchyna was finally returned to Ukraine after months of uncertainty. She was one of 757 Ukrainian casualties handed over by Russian authorities as part of an exchange of prisoners and the dead. Roshchyna had disappeared in the

stories.title: Hungary's next PM says frozen EU funds will be paid out soon
stories.summary: Péter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels, for the first time since his Tisza party won a landslide election.

stories.title: The Guardian view on the politics of central Europe: in search of a new left | Editorial
stories.summary: Social democratic parties are suffering an almost total wipeout, as rightwing nationalism flourishes on the EU’s eastern flank Péter Magyar’s historic defeat of Viktor Orbán in Hungary’s recent election was rightly celebrated in progressive circles and beyond. For the global far right, which has been steadily gaining power and influence for over a decade, this was a significant reverse. But it was no victory for the left. A former member of Mr Orbán’s Fidesz party, Mr Magyar will lead a cen

stories.title: Avalanche deaths have surged in the Alps this season. Here’s why | Johan Gaume
stories.summary: As an ex-pro snowboarder, I know the temptations of fresh snowfall. And as an avalanche researcher, I know how easily people can get into trouble Many avalanche accidents occur just after a storm, when blue-sky conditions return and people head to the backcountry to enjoy fresh powder. These kinds of accidents happen every year, despite warnings to those heading out into the snow. It is easy to dismiss some behaviour as reckless, and sometimes it is – but it is also human behaviour, and it is no

stories.title: Hungary's Magyar meets EU's von der Leyen in high-stakes Brussels visit
stories.summary: Even before Peter Magyar takes office as Hungary's prime minister, he is looking to unlock €10 billion in EU funding ahead of an August deadline. Talks with Ursula von der Leyen come as part of a post-Orban EU reset.

stories.title: Hungary inspires Israeli opposition bid to defeat Netanyahu
stories.summary: Taking a cue from the ousting of Hungary's long-time leader, Viktor Orban, two Israeli opposition figures have joined forces to try to unseat Prime Minister Netanyahu.

stories.title: Horvátország tárgyalásokat kezdeményezne a Mol-INA vita rendezéséről
stories.summary: Magyar Péter a választási győzelme utáni nemzetközi sajtótájékoztatón is kérdést kapott a témában, a horvát N1 televízió Hernádi Zsolt esetleges kiadatásáról érdeklődött.

stories.title: Fekete Giorgio: Az én generációm most először érezheti azt, hogy holnap jobb lehet, mint ma
stories.summary: A Carson Coma énekese szerint most jó építkezés zajlik, de a kormányváltásnak hosszú távon lesz csak hatása. Emellett a közéleti szerepvállalásukról, Bayer Zsoltról és mocskosfideszezésről is beszélt a Telexnek.

stories.title: Meggyanúsították Sára Botondot 70 ezer forint értékű bor elfogadása miatt az Orbán Áront érintő ügyben
stories.summary: A büntetőeljárásban eddig öt embert hallgattak ki gyanúsítottként, a főispán szabadlábon védekezik. Ez annak az ügynek a legújabb fejleménye, amit tavaly nyáron a Telex tárt fel.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a május 25-i héten visszamegy Brüsszelbe, és megállapodást köt az uniós forrásokról
stories.summary: A Brüsszelben tartózkodó leendő kormányfő erről állapodott meg Ursula von der Leyen bizottsági elnökkel.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint a leköszönő kormány nem terjeszti ki a gyanús átutalások felfüggesztésének időtartamát
stories.summary: A leendő miniszterelnök azt is állítja, hogy a kormány eltörölte azt a szabályt, ami szerint a minisztériumok csak a pénzügyminiszter jóváhagyásával költhetnek a keretükön belül.

stories.title: „Az emberek felelőssége, hogy ne történjen egy Fidesz 2”
stories.summary: Németh Hanna matematikus, pókerjátékos, cukrász és a közélettel aktívan foglalkozó influenszer. Célja a kritikus gondolkozás fejlesztése, ne történjen meg újra ugyanaz, mint ami az elmúlt 16 évben volt.

stories.title: Gondokat lát az Európai Bizottság magyar tagjánál az uniós zárszámadás
stories.summary: Várhelyi Olivérnél „elszámoltathatósági, megbízhatósági és jó igazgatásra vonatkozó normákkal” láttak bajokat, a Patrióták Európáért frakción csattanhat 4,3 millió euró jogosulatlan elköltése.

stories.title: Magyar hiányosságokat is felemleget az EP állásfoglalása a 2025-ös jogállamisági helyzetről
stories.summary: A szöveg külön kitért arra, hogy az előző években feltárt, mélyen gyökerező hiányosságok közül számos továbbra is fennáll Magyarországon.

stories.title: Hogyan lehet áron alul eladni egy állami megrendelésekkel kitömött céget?
stories.summary: Azt talán túlzás állítani, hogy a Magyar Péter által megnevezett Lounge Event Kft. értéke nulla, de sokkal közelebb van a nullához, mint ahhoz, ami az eddigi nyereségéből adódna.

stories.title: Kormányváltás kellett ahhoz, hogy a közmédia írjon az ukrán fronton meghalt magyar fiatalról
stories.summary: Aser Benjámin 2025. május 24-én halt meg a harkivi régióban, amiről a közmédia korábban egy cikket sem írt.

stories.title: Nem kivár, előre megy: a Leier válasza az új építőipari korszakra (x)
stories.summary: Bár a statisztikák szerint a magyar építőipar tavaly már enyhe bővülést mutatott, ez nem jelentett automatikusan kedvezőbb üzleti környezetet az építőanyag-gyártóknak. A számok mögött továbbra is erősen tagolt piac áll. Miközben egyes infrastrukturális és ipari projektek lendületet kaptak, a lakossági építkezések és az új családi házak piaca visszafogott maradt. A drága finanszírozás és a kiváró háztartások hatása gyorsan begyűrűzik a gyártókhoz. 2025 inkább stabilizációt, mint fellendülést hozo

stories.title: Merre tart a magyar gazdaság? Ilyen volt az első G7 Paradigma
stories.summary: A G7 Paradigmán cégvezetők, befektetők és közgazdászok beszéltek arról, mi jöhet most, és miben kezd közeledni a gondolkodás.

stories.title: Matolcsy György neve azonnal előkerül, ha elkezdik vizsgálni a spontán privatizációt – de nem úgy
stories.summary: Ő vezette be a közbeszédbe a fogalmat, amelynek hétköznapi értelmezése vitatható módon, de egyenlő lett az állami tulajdon átjátszásával.

stories.title: Két pert is indít az Európai Bizottság a magyar kormány ellen az uniós jog megsértésének gyanúja miatt
stories.summary: A kiskereskedelmiadó-rendszer és az európai elfogatóparancs miatti eljárás már az EU bírósága elé kerül, új ügy lett abból, hogy kiengedik a migránscsempészeket, és az ársapkánál diszkriminálják a külföldieket.

stories.title: Orbán levelet írt a fideszeseknek, szerinte liberális kormányzás és nemzeti ellenzék jön
stories.summary: Kijelölte a politikai irányokat.

stories.title: The Sovereignty Protection Office, which spent billions on communication, took two and a half weeks to speak up after the election
stories.summary: The SPO, established to guard Hungary's sovereignty spent more than two billion forints on communication contracts since 2024. Thought it demanded transparency, it doesn't have a public phone number and very little can be known about its staff. Péter Magyar has vowed to shut it down.

stories.title: The time ahead will be one of liberal governance and a national opposition, Orbán says in letter to Fidesz members
stories.summary: He said that the duty of their community now will be to defend the great national achievements of the past 16 years.

stories.title: Magyar Péter kormánya aligha Tiborcz Istvánnal és Nagy Márton testvérének üzleti köreivel építtetne szélerőműveket
stories.summary: Legalább már vannak fejlesztési tervek 2024 óta, de vajon mi lesz az engedélyezett projektekkel a kormányváltás után? Az építkezések az elmúlt két évben nem indultak el, most pedig minden borulni látszik.

stories.title: Investigation ordered following complaint over detention of Ukrainian cash couriers
stories.summary: According to the lawyer representing the Ukrainian nationals, “the bank's staff were kept in handcuffs and blindfolds for 29 hours, and one of them had to undergo life-saving medical treatment" while the Hungarian authorities detained them in March.

stories.title: Senior Tory MP provided legal services to company of sanctioned Russian oligarchs
stories.summary: LONDON — Former U.K. Attorney General Geoffrey Cox was paid £93,000 to provide legal services to a Luxembourg-based company whose shareholders include two sanctioned Russian oligarchs. Cox, a serving Conservative MP who was the chief legal counsel to former Conservative prime ministers Theresa May and Boris Johnson, has declared 62 hours of work for ABH Holdings S.A. since December last year in the U.K. parliament’s  Register of Members

stories.title: US supreme court conservatives seem to favor ending TPS for Haitians and Syrians
stories.summary: Nine justices were hearing Trump administration that it has authority to strip immigrants’ temporary protected status The US supreme court heard oral arguments on Wednesday over whether the Trump administration can strip the temporary protected status (TPS) of hundreds of thousands of immigrant Haitians and Syrians, under a program that has shielded them from deportation owing to safety concerns in their countries of origin. During the arguments, justices in the conservative-leaning majority app

stories.title: Bernie Sanders urges international cooperation to halt AI’s ‘runaway train’
stories.summary: US senator holds panel with leading Chinese scientists and warns of risks to society unless new technology is regulated The US senator Bernie Sanders espoused the importance of international cooperation in regulating AI at a Wednesday panel on Capitol Hill alongside two leading Chinese scientists. As startups and tech giants, most prominently in Silicon Valley and Beijing, race to advance and scale their artificial intelligence, Sanders has been among the AI skeptics advocating for safeguards. stories.title: Brown University shooting suspect driven by ‘accumulation of grievances’, FBI says
stories.summary: Claudio Neves Valente, who killed himself after deadly attack, began planning for violence in 2022, authorities say The gunman behind a deadly shooting at Brown University in December appeared to have been aggrieved by personal failures and sought retribution against those he deemed responsible, federal authorities said on Wednesday. More than four months after Claudio Manuel Neves Valente opened fire on the Ivy League campus, killing two students and injuring nine others, officials with the FBI

stories.title: US Federal Reserve officials poised to dissent on rate cuts demanded by Trump
stories.summary: A group of Federal Reserve policymakers sent Fed Chair nominee Kevin Warsh a clear warning on Wednesday: They’re not sold on cutting interest rates. The central bank’s rate-setting committee voted to hold borrowing costs steady at what will likely be Jerome Powell’s last meeting as chair. But three regional Fed presidents — a quarter of the central bankers with a vote on policy — dissented on language in the post-meeting statement that suggests the next time the Fed changes rates, it will be to

stories.title: Trump, Putin talk about each other’s wars in Wednesday phone call
stories.summary: President Donald Trump projected confidence that a resolution to the Russia-Ukraine war would come “relatively quickly” after a phone call with President Vladimir Putin Wednesday in which the two leaders discussed their nation’s respective conflicts with Kyiv and Tehran. “I think he’d like to see a solution,” Trump told reporters in an Oval Office press conference. “I can tell you. And that’s good.” Both men are facing difficulties in their country’s respective wars. Russian troops are mired in

stories.title: Ukraine asks Israel to seize vessel it claims is carrying grain stolen by Russia
stories.summary: Accusation vessel contains grain looted from Russian-occupied territories triggers diplomatic spat between both nations Ukraine has asked Israel to seize a vessel it claims is carrying grain looted from Russian-occupied territories, triggering a rare diplomatic spat between the two countries. The dispute spilt into public view this week when president Volodymyr Zelenskyy said that “another vessel” carrying grain “stolen by Russia” had arrived at a port in Israel and was preparing to unload. Cont

stories.title: Police declare attack in north London a terrorist incident after two Jewish men stabbed – as it happened
stories.summary: PM chairs Cobra meeting after condemning ‘appalling antisemitic attack’; man with ‘history of serious violence and mental health issues’ arrested Specialist officers from Counter Terrorism Policing are leading the investigation and working with police to establish the full circumstances and any links to terrorism, the Met said in a statement. Head of counter terrorism policing Laurence Taylor said: Whilst I must stress this investigation is at an early stage, we are working quickly to understand

stories.title: Golders Green attack claim highlights rise of shadowy Iran-linked group
stories.summary: HAYI has taken responsibility for a string of incidents targeting Jewish sites, but investigators say the latest claim may be opportunistic rather than state-backed Police treating stabbing of two men in Golders Green as terrorism It took just over an hour after the horrific knife attack on two British Jewish people in Golders Green, north London, for an Iran-linked terror group, Harakat Ashab al-Yamin al-Islamia (HAYI), to make a claim of responsibility on a Telegram channel. Counter-terror pol

stories.title: Raise tax on alcohol and junk food to cut deaths from liver disease, experts say
stories.summary: Report calls for tough action to combat ‘escalating and unsustainable burden’ of liver-related problems in Europe Governments in Europe should impose much higher taxes on alcohol and unhealthy food to tackle the continent’s 284,000 deaths a year from liver disease, experts say. Taxes on those products should rise sharply enough for the money raised to cover the huge costs they place on health services, the criminal justice system and social services. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump threatens to reduce troop numbers in Germany amid growing row with Nato allies
stories.summary: US president’s threat comes after Germany’s Friedrich Merz suggests Trump team is being outplayed in its negotiations with Iran The US may reduce its number of troops deployed in Germany, Donald Trump has announced, days after the country’s chancellor said America was being “humiliated” by Iran. In a post on his Truth Social platform, the US president said his administration was “studying and reviewing the possible reduction of troops in Germany, with a determination to be made over the next sho

stories.title: Florida approves US House map meant to boost Republicans in midterms
stories.summary: Vote comes on same day the US supreme court rolls back a key provision of the Voting Rights Act The Florida legislature approved a new congressional map intended to maximize Republicans’ advantage in the state as part of the national redistricting battle that Donald Trump launched before this year’s midterms. The vote came just two days after the governor, Ron DeSantis , unveiled his proposal and the same day the US supreme court rolled back a key provision of the Voting Rights Act. The decision

stories.title: World’s largest aircraft carrier to return to US after record deployment
stories.summary: USS Gerald R Ford to sail home after 10-month spell including role in Maduro capture and Middle East war The world’s largest aircraft carrier, the USS Gerald R Ford, will be heading home following a record-setting deployment of more than 300 days that included participating in the war against Iran and capture of Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro, two US officials said Wednesday. The Ford will be leaving the Middle East in the coming days and returning to its home port in Virginia in mid-May, acco

stories.title: US charges Sinaloa governor and other Mexican officials with drug trafficking offences
stories.summary: Indictment accuses high-level officials in Sinaloa of offences such as drug trafficking, weapons offences and kidnapping The US justice department has charged the governor of Sinaloa and nine other current and former officials for alleged ties to the Sinaloa cartel, accusing them of aiding in the massive importation of illicit narcotics into the United States . Some officials were members of Mexico’s progressive ruling party, Morena, posing a political conundrum for Mexican president Claudia She

stories.title: Kim praises North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up to evade Ukraine capture
stories.summary: Leader mentions for first time lengths to which troops go to avoid falling into enemy hands while fighting for Russia Kim Jong-un has praised North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up with grenades in order to avoid capture while fighting Ukrainian forces in Russia’s western Kursk region, confirming the existence of the extreme battlefield policy. Mounting evidence, including from intelligence reports and testimonies of defectors, has indicated North Korean soldiers are explicitly told to res

stories.title: More private health records of UK Biobank volunteers appear on Chinese website
stories.summary: Patrick Vallance says government working with Chinese officials to remove postings from Alibaba after Biobank data breach last week There have been further listings of confidential health records of UK volunteers on the Chinese website Alibaba since the breach reported last week, and the government is braced for further leaks, the science minister has said. Addressing a House of Lords debate on the attempted sale of data belonging to 500,000 UK Biobank volunteers, Patrick Vallance said the gover

stories.title: South Africa deports Mugabe’s son for unrelated offences after employee shot at family home
stories.summary: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe also fined after pleading guilty to immigration and firearms-related offences Two months after an employee was shot in the back at the Mugabe family home in a wealthy suburb of Johannesburg, a South African court has fined and ordered the deportation of Robert Mugabe’s youngest son over two unrelated charges. Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, 28, and his cousin Tobias Mugabe Matonhodze, 33, were initially both charged with attempted murder after the incident on 19 February.

stories.title: USA: Ex-FBI chief Comey appears in court over Trump threat
stories.summary: Comey is charged with acting "knowingly and willfully" to threaten the life of the president and of transmitting threats across state lines. His attorney says the case ​is a "vindictive prosecution."

stories.title: Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say
stories.summary: They are among nine people killed in successive attacks on Tuesday, which Israel's military says targeted a Hezbollah commander.

stories.title: New images show suspect taking selfies before Washington press dinner shooting
stories.summary: Prosecutors argue Cole Tomas Allen should remain in custody until trial on a charge of attempted assassination of President Donald Trump.

stories.title: Madagascar detains French national over alleged plot to stir unrest
stories.summary: Malagasy prosecuters used a Whatsapp group as evidence for the detained individuals alleged crimes.

stories.title: At least five killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza including a nine-year-old boy
stories.summary: Eighty-one sick and injured children accompanied by their families were evacuated from Gaza to receive medical treatment in Jordan. They travelled via the King Hussein Bridge to various government and private hospitals in the capital Amman.

stories.title: Irish government announces further fuel supports after protests
stories.summary: The Irish government has announced further fuel supports for a number of sectors after nationwide protests in recent weeks.

stories.title: Robert Mugabe's son to be deported from South Africa over firearms offence
stories.summary: The 28-year-old was arrested in February after a man had been shot at his home in Johannesburg.

stories.title: EU farmers and hauliers to get up to €50,000 to cover extra costs of Iran war
stories.summary: Fishing companies can also access subsidies in loosening of state aid rules to cover fuel and fertiliser price rises The EU is to subsidise up to 70% of the extra cost of fuel and fertilisers caused by the Iran war for farmers, fishing businesses and road hauliers as part of a package of emergency measures. Individual companies can claim up to €50,000 each between now and the end of the year with minimum paperwork, a measure the EU hopes will remove what it sees as an existential threat to hauli

stories.title: Thousands of US hockey fans sing Canadian anthem amid tensions between neighbor countries
stories.summary: Fans in Buffalo, only a few miles from Ontario, filled the silence when a microphone cut out at the start of a match The Electric City. Nickel City. Queen City. City of No Illusions. Buffalo, New York, has accrued many nicknames over the years but, in an age of growing tensions between two traditional allies, one among them has taken on extra resonance: the City of Good Neighbors. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Month-long mission to save animal in Baltic sea off Germany has attracted national frenzy Timmy the whale rescue attempt begins off coast of Germany – in pictures Attempts to rescue a young humpback whale stranded in shallow waters off the Baltic coast in Germany have been criticised by the International Whaling Commission (IWC) as “inadvisable”. stories.title: Family of ailing Iranian Nobel laureate say keeping her in jail is a death sentence
stories.summary: Narges Mohammadi denied medical leave from prison in spite of sharp decline in health and drastic weight loss, say lawyers The family of the jailed Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi say they fear for her life after a sharp deterioration in her health, suspected heart attack and drop in body weight of almost 20kg (44lb). The 54-year-old human rights activist, who was awarded the 2023 Nobel peace prize while in prison , had been released for health reasons in 2024. She was re-arrested in Dec

stories.title: GB News commentator to sue charity for not offering internships to white people
stories.summary: Sophie Corcoran challenging 10,000 Interns Foundation, which works with people from under-represented groups An influencer is taking a charity that organises internships for black and minority ethnic people to court because they do not organise schemes for white people. Sophie Corcoran, a GB News commentator, applied to a programme the 10,000 Interns Foundation was running with the Bar Council. She said she was “shocked to discover that the scheme is restricted to applicants of a particular raci

stories.title: Knee surgery for cartilage damage does not benefit patients, study suggests
stories.summary: People with meniscus tears who underwent surgery had poorer knee function and worse osteoarthritis after 10 years than those who did not A common knee surgery for cartilage damage does not benefit patients and may lead to worse outcomes, a 10-year trial suggests. The study tracked outcomes for patients treated for a meniscus tear, who were given a partial meniscectomy, one of the most common orthopaedic surgeries. Their trajectories were compared with patients who had randomly been assigned to r

stories.title: Stephen Fry sues tech conference organisers for £100,000 over fall from stage
stories.summary: Actor and presenter broke his hip, right leg, pelvis and ribs when he gave a talk at CogX festival at O2 Arena in 2023 Stephen Fry is suing two companies that organised a tech conference where he was injured in 2023 after falling off the stage, high court documents show. The actor and presenter broke his hip and had multiple breaks in his right leg, pelvis and ribs when he attended the CogX festival at the O2 Arena, where he delivered a talk on artificial intelligence on 14 September 2023. stories.title: Trial of non-invasive endometriosis scan boosts hopes for quicker diagnosis
stories.summary: Results suggest radiotracer maraciclatide can ‘light up’ condition on scan and reduce need for investigative surgery A non-invasive scan for endometriosis has shown promising results in a trial, boosting hopes for far quicker diagnosis. The trial, which included 19 women with the condition, suggests that an experimental radiotracer, called maraciclatide, can “light up” endometriosis on a scan. stories.title: Anthony Albanese rules out gas export tax on existing contracts and criticises ‘populist’ campaign
stories.summary: Prime minister says the middle of a global fuel crisis is ‘the worst possible time to jeopardise’ Australia’s partnerships with Asian trading partners Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese has confirmed next month’s federal budget will not include a new tax on existing gas export contracts as he criticised the “populist” campaign calling for a levy on producers. As reported last week , the prime minist

stories.title: Families sue OpenAI over failure to report Canada mass shooter’s behavior on ChatGPT
stories.summary: New lawsuits allege employees urged company to notify authorities months before deadly Tumbler Ridge attack Families of seven victims of a mass shooting at a secondary school in British Columbia are suing OpenAI and the company’s CEO for negligence after it failed to alert authorities to the shooter’s troubling conversations with ChatGPT. The lawsuits, filed on Wednesday in a federal court in San Francisco, allege that the violent intentions of the shooter, identified as 18-year-old Jesse Van Ro

stories.title: ‘Total peace’ or ‘all-out war’? Colombian voters face stark choice as rebel attacks surge
stories.summary: As the country prepares to elect a new president, a fierce debate is raging on how to end the decades-long armed conflict for good The landmark 2016 peace deal between the Colombian government and the largest insurgent army in Latin America succeeded in some ways: the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc) agreed to lay down their weapons, and the violence that had racked the country was substantially reduced. But the deal alone could not end the decades-long armed conflict for good. Subs

stories.title: Reliance on Chinese green tech poses ‘serious’ risk for Europe, experts say
stories.summary: Continent ‘sleepwalking’ into series of economic and national security problems due to over-dependence Europe is “sleepwalking” into a series of economic and national security problems because of an over-reliance on Chinese green technology, according to experts. A report co-authored by Michael Collins, a former deputy head of national security strategy at the UK Cabinet Office, described the risks of depending on China for green tech as “serious”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Why Ghana walked away from a US health deal
stories.summary: Talks between Ghana and the US over a bilateral healthcare deal have stalled, after Accra voiced concerns regarding sensitive data sharing. Several African countries have pushed back on the deals, while some have signed.

stories.title: Germany: Merz hails 'historic' health care reform
stories.summary: The Cabinet has greenlit a health care draft law designed to lower insurance rates. The bill heads to parliament next, but doctors' associations and patient protection advocates say it amounts to austerity measures.

stories.title: Russia to hold Victory Day parade without weaponry display
stories.summary: The scaled back parade comes amid increasing Ukrainian attacks deep into mainland Russia, including the capital Moscow. The last time a Victory Day parade was held without weaponry display was in 2007.

stories.title: Teenage bullying increases in Ukrainian schools
stories.summary: The number of teenage victims of bullying is rising in Ukrainian schools. Experts attribute this to war trauma and chronic stress among teenagers.

stories.title: Mali: France urges citizens to leave amid Tuareg advance
stories.summary: With Tuareg-led rebels saying the ruling junta in Mali will fall "sooner or later" and demanding the withdrawal of Russian troops, the French Foreign Ministry has called on French nationals to leave the country.

stories.title: What are the sticking points for the EU's new budget?
stories.summary: The battle over the next long-term EU budget has begun. The EU's institutions have staked out their positions and are seeking significantly more funding, but it is the 27 member states that will ultimately decide.

stories.title: US top court mulls scrapping Syrians', Haitians' protection
stories.summary: The Trump administration is appealing lower court orders that blocked it from immediately ending temporary protected status for people from Haiti and Syria.

stories.title: King Charles commemorates 9/11 victims in New York visit
stories.summary: The royal couple laid a bouquet at the September 11 Memorial and met with victim's families.

stories.title: Trump threatens US troop cuts in Germany
stories.summary: Donald Trump says the US is considering a "possible reduction" of its forces in Germany just days after German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the US was being 'humilitated' by Iran's leadership.

stories.title: Whale 'Timmy' reaches Denmark in rescue effort — in pictures
stories.summary: A barge carrying a humpback whale stranded in Germany for weeks has reached Danish waters on its journey to the North Sea.

stories.title: UK: London stabbing being treated as 'terrorist incident'
stories.summary: UK police have declared the stabbing of two men in a traditionally Jewish area of London as a "terrorist incident." The victims are in a stable condition. Prime Minister Starmer called the incident "deeply concerning."

stories.title: US Supreme Court limits use of race in drawing electoral maps
stories.summary: The decision will make it more difficult to successfully challenge legislative maps for diluting the voting power of racial minorities.

stories.title: France investigates reappearance of website linked to Pelicot crimes
stories.summary: Authorities say the Coco platform has been linked to sexual abuse of children, drug offences, rape and murder.

stories.title: 'Numbskull, moron and too stupid': Trump and Powell's biggest clashes
stories.summary: How the US President Donald Trump and Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell came to blows.

stories.title: In five charts - How UAE's exit could affect Opec's influence over the oil price
stories.summary: The BBC takes a look in charts at what the UAE's departure could mean for the oil cartel and more widely.

stories.title: France urges citizens to leave Mali after rebel attacks
stories.summary: The UK has issued similar advice, telling citizens who stay, that they do so at their own risk.

stories.title: Stranded whale ferried out of German waters in barge
stories.summary: The final operation to save the whale is being closely followed, after the failure of earlier attempts.

stories.title: Seven lawsuits filed against OpenAI by families of Canada mass-shooting victims
stories.summary: The lawsuits, filed in California, accuse OpenAI and Sam Altman of negligence and abetting a mass shooting by failing to flag the suspect's ChatGPT activity.

stories.title: Four key takeaways from Jerome Powell's final rate decision as Fed chair
stories.summary: Powell's news conference after holding rates covered the US-Israel war with Iran, inflation, legal attacks, and the Fed's independence.

stories.title: Syria trial seen as first step on long road for Assad's victims
stories.summary: There were highly charged scenes as former senior regime official, Atef Najib, appeared before a Damascus court.

stories.title: Comey's seashell post got him indicted. But experts are sceptical the government can win
stories.summary: Acting AG Todd Blanche said the case was investigated for months, adding "it's serious when you threaten the president."

stories.title: Comey surrenders over charge of threatening Trump's life in Instagram post
stories.summary: Prosecutors say a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director was a call for violence against Trump.

stories.title: Video shows suspect approaching police after stabbing in north London
stories.summary: Images shared on social media show police tasering and arresting a man with a knife in Golders Green in north London, after two Jewish men were stabbed in an attack. Police declared it a "terrorist incident" and said a 45-year-old man had been arrested, adding that he had also tried to stab officers

stories.title: Florida lawmakers approve new voting maps to favour Republicans
stories.summary: The state is the latest to act in a national redistricting war that could shape what political party controls the US House of Representatives.

stories.title: Hegseth clashes with lawmakers over Iran war in nearly six-hour-long hearing
stories.summary: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth sparred with Democratic lawmakers during a nearly six-hour-long hearing, in his first time facing questions under oath since the start of the Iran war.

stories.title: Oil price rises above $120 after reports of 'extended' Iran blockade
stories.summary: The price of crude oil has swung sharply as uncertainty over the war in the Middle East continues.

stories.title: A son overlooked and a jailed tycoon: Inside Samsung's succession drama
stories.summary: The family dynasty behind Samsung is so complicated it regularly makes headline news in South Korea.

stories.title: King and Queen lay flowers at 9/11 Memorial in New York
stories.summary: King Charles and Queen Camilla on their state visit to the US visit the memorial in New York.

stories.title: The 'Polar Bear Capital' with Arctic gateway ambitions
stories.summary: Canada is weighing whether a year-round northern trade route to Europe from Churchill, Manitoba, is viable, as climate change speeds Arctic warming.

stories.title: Indians lost $25bn to digital fraud in 2025 - now its central bank is fighting back
stories.summary: The RBI is mulling measures to tackle rising cases of digital fraud in India - but will they work?

stories.title: 'I did not expect it': Kenya's Sabastian Sawe welcomed home with jubilant celebrations
stories.summary: The first man to run a marathon in under two hours in a competitive race received a hero's welcome from supporters and family when he arrived home.

stories.title: US charges Mexican governor and other leaders with aiding drug cartel
stories.summary: The governor of Sinaloa, Rúben Rocha Moya, hails from the same party as the president of Mexico.

stories.summary: A severe storm outbreak has been battering parts of the Midwest and Southern United States for nearly a week.

stories.title: US singer D4vd bought tools online to dispose of girl's body, prosecutors allege
stories.summary: The Tiktok star's attorneys have maintained his innocence and vowed to "vigorously defend" him.

stories.title: 'Attacked 28 times in a day' - BBC visits heavily targeted US-UK base in Iraq
stories.summary: Before the fragile ceasefire in the region, this was one of the most heavily targeted US and UK military bases in the Middle East.

stories.title: Joy as record-breaking runner Sawe returns home
stories.summary: BBC reports from Kenya, home to Sabastian Sawe, the first man to run a marathon in under two hours.

stories.title: Bondi shooting inquiry calls for gun reform and more security at Jewish festivals

stories.summary: Israel has been fighting Hezbollah since early March, sending troops into south Lebanon to battle the Iran-backed militant group, with the violence ongoing despite a shaky ongoing ceasefire.

stories.summary: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, a powerful figure, has grown in prominence since the start of the war and was the lead negotiator in the so far only round of direct US-Iranian talks.

stories.title: US has 'no closer friends than the British,' Trump tells King Charles III at White House ceremony
stories.summary: Trump’s up-and-down relationship with UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has taken a particularly sour turn over the past several months as the president has sought to rally international support for the Iran war.

stories.title: Former US officials criticise Pentagon silence on deadly Iran school attack
stories.summary: In the two months since the deadly strike, the US defence department has said only that the incident is under investigation.

stories.title: Births, deaths and a first kiss: life near the frontline in Ukraine – in pictures
stories.summary: Aria Shahrokhshahi went to teenage discos and hospital wards rattled by rockets in order to capture how it feels when your country is dragged into a war Continue reading...

stories.title: King Charles agrees with me on Iran nuclear weapon ban, claims Trump
stories.summary: Remarks by US president likely to cause embarrassment for aides of UK monarch, who usually remains neutral UK politics live – latest updates Donald Trump has claimed King Charles agrees with him that Iran should never be allowed nuclear weapons. Trump made the remarks at a White House state dinner on Tuesday in honour of the visiting Charles and Camilla, after the two men sat down to bilateral talks earlier that day. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany news: Cabinet discusses 2027 budget plans as Iran war threatens finances
stories.summary: The Cabinet hopes to sign off on core points of its 2027 budget, so it can start to move through parliament. Germany already plans massive borrowing next year, before adequately factoring in Iran-based costs. Follow DW.

stories.title: Why UAE's OPEC exit is a blow to Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates is leaving OPEC to pump more oil on its own terms. The break strips Saudi Arabia of a key partner and adds to growing uncertainty over the cartel's future.

stories.title: 'Rose' and the piece of fabric that embodies gender politics
stories.summary: As the film starring Sandra Hüller is released in Germany, DW talked to director Markus Schleinzer about why women passing as men inspired his historical drama, and why it's a current topic.

stories.title: Iran war: Brent oil briefly hits $119 as talks stall
stories.summary: The price of Brent crude oil has soared as Iran continues to block traffic through the Strait of Hormuz amid a US blockade on its ports. The Pentagon put the cost of war at about $25 billion. Follow DW for the latest.

stories.title: Why Germany wants a seat at the UN Security Council
stories.summary: With the rules-based world order "under stress," Germany is on a mission to gain a coveted seat at the United Nations' top table. German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul told DW why diplomacy still matters.

stories.title: Iran war squeezes India's pharma supply chain
stories.summary: Supply chain disruptions, higher energy and freight prices and reliance on Chinese pharmaceutical ingredients are squeezing margins as well as raising the risk of higher drug prices and potential shortages.

stories.title: Afghanistan: Shiite and other minorities living in fear
stories.summary: An attack on a Shiite place of worship in Herat killed least 11 people. While the Taliban have pledged protection, a sense of structural vulnerability is growing within the Shiite community.

stories.title: 2026 Goethe Medal: Meet the recipients
stories.summary: Though they work in different fields and countries, what unites them is their focus on a Europe in transition: Arvo Pärt, Anita Raja and Prodromos Tsinikoris have been awarded the 2026 Goethe Medal.

stories.title: Firetrucks swarm Cologne Cathedral after smell of smoke
stories.summary: A large number of firetrucks were mobilized to the Cologne Cathedral after a churchwarden reported the smell of smoke.

stories.title: Watch: Key moments from King Charles III’s address to US Congress
stories.summary: King Charles III addressed US Congress, stressing the importance of the UK-US partnership in facing global challenges. Lawmakers applauded as he mixed humor, history and forward-looking remarks in a rare speech by a British monarch.

stories.title: Greenpeace stages beach protest against fossil fuels during Colombia conference
stories.summary: Greenpeace activists displayed large messages on a beach in Colombia, calling for a transition away from fossil fuels during an international conference.

stories.title: Elderly woman rescued by autonomous robot near Ukraine frontline
stories.summary: Video released by Ukraine’s Third Army Corps shows a four-hour rescue in Lyman, where a 77-year-old woman was saved by an autonomous vehicle after being spotted by drone pilots. The ground drone carried a blanket and a note reading: “Grandma, sit down!”

stories.title: Two dead after small plane crashes into Australia airport hangar
stories.summary: The aircraft burst into flames after it crashed at Parafield Airport in Adelaide.

stories.title: Hundreds gather to smell rare ‘corpse flower’ blooming in London
stories.summary: Visitors flocked to Kew Gardens in London to witness and smell the rare blooming of the titan arum, known for its foul odour.

stories.title: US opioid crisis: families speak out after Purdue Pharma sentencing
stories.summary: Relatives of opioid overdose victims reacted outside a US court after Purdue Pharma was sentenced over its role in the opioid crisis.

stories.title: Russian paramilitaries carried out air strikes in Mali as rebels advanced, footage shows
stories.summary: Last weekend's attacks came as the Africa Corps withdrew from a key base in northern Mali.

stories.title: Iran executed 21 people and arrested thousands since start of war, UN warns
stories.summary: UN has warned that the Tehran regime has executed 21 people and detained over 4,000 since the war began, accusing the Islamic Republic of torture, disappearances and targeting minorities.

stories.title: Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine
stories.summary: Russia says the annual commemoration of Soviet victory in World War Two will not feature military vehicles or cadets.

stories.title: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
stories.summary: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

stories.title: Gulf unity on display in Jeddah, but absences and signals reveal limits to coordination
stories.summary: Gulf leaders met in Saudi Arabia to address the fallout from the Iran war, projecting unity but stopping short of concrete steps as divisions and uncertainty persist.

stories.title: Watch: There's a ceasefire in Lebanon - but the fighting hasn't stopped
stories.summary: Israel and Hezbollah are still fighting in Lebanon despite a US-brokered ceasefire that began 12 days ago.

stories.title: Három férfit azzal vádolnak, hogy egy online profil kérésére gyújtották fel a brit miniszterelnök két házát
stories.summary: A profil állítólag pénzt ajánlott a férfiaknak, akik tavaly május közepén gyújtogattak.

stories.title: A Szociális és Gyermekvédelmi Főigazgatóság korrupt vezetéséről beszélt egy gyermekvédelmi vezető, a főigazgatóság szinte minden állításáért perel
stories.summary: A Tolna Megyei Gyermekvédelmi Központ korábbi vezetője azt mondta, az SZGYF bizalma akkor rendült meg benne, amikor nem akart elfogadni egy túlárazott, 28 milliós ajánlatot egy tetőszerkezet javítására.

stories.title: Geszti Péter nem vágyik pozícióra a közmédiában, de szívesen beszélgetne emberekkel a témáról
stories.summary: Pikó András józsefvárosi polgármester és egykori újságíró és Boros Ilona, a TASZ jogásza is vendégek voltak Krug Emília új műsorában.

stories.title: Nagyon nehéz helyzetbe kerülhet az MCC, ha nem fizet nekik osztalékot sem a Mol, sem a Richter
stories.summary: Legnagyobb bevételi forrásaitól eshet el a fideszes káderképző, ha nem kapnak a két cég osztalékaiból. Majdnem 37 milliárd forintra számítanak, de a Mol és a Richter is elhalasztotta a kifizetéseket.

stories.title: Markó Béla: Az RMDSZ eltávolodott a közösségtől, ki kell nyitni, új szövetséget kell építeni
stories.summary: Az RMDSZ az elmúlt években egyre inkább eltávolodott attól a közösségtől, amelyet képviselnie kellene – figyelmeztetett Markó Béla a Transtelex Dialógon. Szerinte a Szövetség beszűkült, elpártosodott működése miatt elveszítette korábbi pluralizmusát, ezért nem elég részleges korrekciókban gondolkodni: ki kell nyitni a szervezetet, újra kell építeni a bizalmat, és egy szélesebb, inkluzívabb alapokon működő új szövetséget kell létrehozni.

stories.title: 25 milliárd dollárba került eddig az iráni háború az Egyesült Államoknak
stories.summary: Azt nem közölték, ebbe az összegbe beleszámolták-e a Közel-Keleten megrongálódott katonai infrastruktúra helyreállításának költségeit.

stories.summary: Az érintett óvodapedagógus már felmondott. Az intézményben tartott belső meghallgatáson a gyermekvédelmi felelős az igazgató férje volt, de a fenntartó szerint ez nem releváns az ügy szempontjából.

stories.summary: A reformra szinte egyhangúlag mondtak igent.

stories.summary: A Truth Socialre arról posztolt, hogy nem lesz többet jó fiú.

stories.title: Két kamion ütközött, Szeged felé lezárták az M5-öst

stories.title: Majdnem elsírta magát a jászberényi önkormányzat fideszes képviselője, amikor arról beszélt, megalázó, hogy Pócs János megint a parlamentben ülhet

stories.title: Humanoid robots to become baggage handlers in Japan airport experiment
stories.summary: Japan Airlines will introduce the robots for trial run at a Tokyo airport amid country’s surge in inbound tourism and worsening labour shortages Japan’s famously conscientious but overburdened baggage handlers will soon be joined by extra staff at Tokyo’s Haneda airport – although their new colleagues will need to take regular recharging breaks. Japan Airlines will introduce humanoid robots on a trial basis from the beginning of May, with a view to deploying them permanently as a solution to the

stories.title: Sri Lanka police arrest 22 Buddhist monks after 110kg of cannabis found in luggage
stories.summary: Customs officials say group allegedly hid 5kg of ‘kush’ in false walls of bags on return from Bangkok holiday Twenty-two Buddhist monks are in Sri Lankan police custody after customs officials found 110kg of high-grade cannabis concealed in their luggage, the largest ever drug bust at Colombo’s main international airport. Hezbollah claimed Tuesday’s attack injured several Israeli soldiers, but no confirmation was given from the Israeli military, apart from a s

stories.title: Trumps greet King Charles III and Queen Camilla at the White House

stories.title: Dozens of arrests made in Turkey ahead of 1 May celebrations, Istanbul prosecutor says
stories.summary: The day, which celebrates workers and the working classes, usually sees a major police deployment in Turkey every year.

stories.title: Afghanistan says Pakistani strikes kill seven and wound 85 in first attack since peace talks

stories.title: One year after Spain’s blackout, its shift to renewables and grid evolution power on
stories.summary: Though solar was initially incorrectly blamed for crisis, renewables have helped insulate Spain from gas price rises caused by war in Middle East One year ago today, all of Spain, and much of Portugal, suffered through a blackout of unprecedented scale and duration. In mere seconds, a cascading sequence of events burst through the grid and created Europe’s first “system black” event in recent memory. Traffic signals failed, mobile networks stopped working entirely, petrol stations could not pump

stories.title: Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week
stories.summary: Pirates appear to be taking advantage of international naval strength being diverted to Middle East Three vessels have been hijacked off the coast of Somalia in the past week, raising fears of a resurgence in piracy around the Horn of Africa, and adding to the woes of the global shipping industry. The merchant vessel Sward was taken over on 26 April, a day after a dhow was seized. These followed the 21 April hijacking of Honour 25, a motor tanker carrying 18,000 barrels of oil, according to the

stories.title: How the UAE’s decision to leave Opec could recast the Middle East
stories.summary: Defection is damaging to Saudi Arabia’s prestige – and could strengthen the US hand in the region The United Arab Emirates’ decision to walk out of Opec is a political as much as business decision, and will reignite the simmering rows between the UAE and Saudi Arabia – which had been covered up by their shared anger with Iran over its attacks on the Gulf states since the start of the US-Israel war on Tehran. In the short term, leaving the oil producing cartel it joined in 1967 gives the UAE the

stories.title: Hét perc alatt, a nyílt utcán varrta össze egy férfi szívét az Onkológiai Intézet légimentős főorvosa
stories.summary: A főorvos épp a légimentőknél volt ügyeletes, amikor egy szívtájékon szúrt férfihoz riasztották őket.

stories.title: Galambokra vadászó férfi lőtt be véletlenül a solymári óvoda épületébe
stories.summary: A lövedék áttörte az egyik csoportszoba lehúzott redőnyét.

stories.title: Érdemtelenségre hivatkozva kirúgta a választás előtt kitálaló vezető közgazdászát a GVH
stories.summary: Erről LinkedIn profilján számolt be Berezvai Zombor, aki korábban azt állította, a GVH indoklás nélkül leállította a NER-közeli cégeknél indított vizsgálatokat.

stories.title: Bűncselekmény hiányában nem nyomoznak tovább a pap ügyében, akit korábban eltiltottak a hittanoktatástól Kecskeméten
stories.summary: Az a gyanú merült fel, hogy nem megengedett módon érhetett hozzá diákokhoz.

stories.title: A Bárczi dékánja szerint ősszel fogják bemutatni az autizmus spektrum pedagógiája szakirány új oktatói csapatát
stories.summary: Levélben igyekezett megnyugtatni a hallgatókat dr. Papp Gabriella, a Bárczi Gusztáv Gyógypedagógiai Karának dékánja.

stories.title: Az önkormányzat testületi üléseit ajánlotta a fogyatékkal élők igényeinek kielégítésére a dörgicsei önkormányzat a helyi esélyegyenlőségi programban
stories.summary: „Időnként az Önkormányzat testületi ülésén megrendezésre kerül az ún. »Maunika só«”, ami a trash reality kedvelőinek igényeit elégíti ki – írták a programban.

stories.title: Úgy nőtt fel egy generáció, hogy folyamatosan hülyének voltak nézve – interjú Fekete Giorgióval
stories.summary: Sokszor beleálltak a rendszerbe, és sok savat is kaptak a nyakukba ezért. Szerinte sokszor csak arról lehetett dönteni, hogy egy vagy két lépésre kerül valaki közel a NER-hez. Hogyan élte meg a választás napját, és mit vár az új világtól? Élőben Fekete Giorgio, a Carson Coma frontembere.

stories.title: Nincs mit beismernem – mondta a Till Tamás meggyilkolásával gyanúsított férfi a bíróságon
stories.summary: F. János szerint kényszerítették, hogy beismerje a bűnösségét. Az ügyész 15 év fegyházbüntetést kért rá, és azt, hogy feltételesen se bocsássák szabadlábra.

stories.title: Hulmánokkal, bali seregélyekkel és egy több mint 14 méter magas vízeséssel nyílhat meg nyár közepén a Biodóm egy része
stories.summary: Igazi négyévszakos látnivalót kínál majd a látogatóknak a városi oázis, ami a terv szerint július 1-én nyílhat majd meg.

stories.title: Két zsidó férfit megkéseltek Londonban, a támadó késsel üldözte az embereket, többeket is megpróbált megszúrni
stories.summary: A rendőrség végül sokkolóval ártalmatlanította a támadót, aki a helyi zsidó közösség tagjait vette célba.

stories.title: Egyetlen nap alatt 51 bankszámláját szüntette meg az MNB-botrány emblematikus cége
stories.summary: Ez ráadásul már a harmadik ilyen kör volt: másfél hónap alatt 84 számlát zártak le. Elég nehezen érthető, hogy miért volt szüksége ennyi bankszámlára a Raw Development Kft.-nek.

stories.title: Menczer: Én minden munkámmal, minden szavammal és tettemmel el tudok számolni
stories.summary: A Fidesz kommunikációs igazgatója a 444-nek arról beszélt, szerinte az őt egyéni választókerületében nagyon megverő Bujdosó Andreának semmi köze nincs a győzelemhez.

stories.title: Nagy munka vár a kultuszminiszterre, akitől nemcsak korrekciót, hanem teljes újragondolást várnak
stories.summary: Előadóművészeket, múzeumi dolgozókat, műkritikusokat kérdeztünk Tarr Zoltán kinevezéséről, akinek nemcsak a neve, hanem a tárcájának létezése is meglepetés lehetett.

stories.title: Germany news: Major raids carried out against Hells Angels
stories.summary: A large-scale police operation in the western state of North Rhine-Westphalia has targeted biker gang crime. Meanwhile, Germany is to bid again for a non-permanent seat in the UN Security Council.

stories.title: 'Marine unicorns' aren't loving Arctic noise
stories.summary: Narwhals are fleeing Canada's far north. Researchers suspect a link to noise pollution from increasing ship traffic.

stories.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy rebukes Israel for buying grain 'stolen' by Russia
stories.summary: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has accused Israel of "purchasing stolen goods," namely grain from regions occupied by Russia. Ukraine summoned Israel's ambassador on Monday, and now it says another such ship has docked.

stories.title: What Renault's new EV reveals about the global auto industry
stories.summary: French carmaker Renault no longer sells its brand in the Chinese market, but it's not afraid to design and develop them there. It's just one more sign of big shifts in the global auto industry.

stories.title: Germany's entrepreneurs betting on post-Maduro Venezuela
stories.summary: Venezuela's economy is opening sectors to foreign firms, and that could pay off for the few German companies that have stuck it out through years of decline.

stories.title: How a controversial gas pipeline deal could further stall Bosnia's EU accession
stories.summary: A US backed gas pipeline could help Bosnia cut reliance on Russian energy. But Brussels warns that a deal with a company linked to Trump allies could put the country's EU hopes at risk.

stories.title: 'Only yes means yes' rape definition backed by EU lawmakers
stories.summary: The European Parliament voted for a consent-based definition of rape. But what constitutes rape under criminal law still varies significantly across the bloc.

stories.title: Extreme weather and green energy on the rise in Europe
stories.summary: Europe's climate extremes have hit new highs — but renewables are now supplying nearly half of the continent's electricity.

stories.title: King Charles III welcomed at the White House as four-day state visit begins
stories.summary: The state visit to mark the 250th anniversary of US independence comes amid a strain in the UK - US special relationship following Trump's repeated bashing of British Prime Minister Keir Starmer over the US war with Iran.

stories.title: Rare spring snowstorm hits Moscow
stories.summary: A rare spring snowfall blankets Moscow, forcing flight cancellations and toppling trees. The Russian Meteorological Center has declared an orange alert level for the capital, in effect until Tuesday morning.

stories.title: Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz if US lifts blockade, reports claim
stories.summary: Despite an ongoing ceasefire, the US and Iran are locked in a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of the world’s oil and gas trade passes in peacetime.

stories.title: Iran parades armed women in streets as questions over AI videos grow
stories.summary: Videos emerging on social media from Iran appear to show armed Iranian women parading with IRGC in several cities in an upsurge of nationalist and religious fervour, dubbed as a “jihad of women”, but some clips spark AI editing doubts and remain unverified.

stories.title: Mexican army captures top cartel boss in another blow to Jalisco New Generation
stories.summary: The Mexican army has captured CJNG leader Audias Flores Silva aka "The Gardener," a key "El Mencho" lieutenant, dealing another blow to the powerful cartel amid ongoing violence.

stories.title: Miners’ hunger strike in Turkey enters second week
stories.summary: A hunger strike by workers protesting months of unpaid wages and alleged rights violations entered its second week on April 27 in Ankara, Turkey.

stories.title: Louis Arnaud, former French hostage in Iran, describes Evin prison as 'the den of evil'
stories.summary: Speaking to Euronews, Louis Arnaud says the memories of Evin Prison continue to haunt him, as he recalls a place where captives are crammed into windowless cells and where the lights never go out.

stories.title: UAE leaves OPEC, citing national interest in 'a new energy age'
stories.summary: The UAE’s historic decision is a major blow to the alliance of oil producers, just as the global energy crisis is escalating over Iran’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, but the Gulf state insists its decision “enhances the UAE’s ability to respond to evolving market needs."

stories.title: Qatar warns Iran it will not be used as 'political punching bag'
stories.summary: Qatar warned Iran on Tuesday it will not be used as a “political punching bag,” calling for a "comprehensive deal" that would secure the Gulf. In response to Euronews, Qatar's foreign ministry spokesman said "we applaud our partners in Europe for taking the lead" in the Strait of Hormuz crisis.

stories.title: Faisal Islam: Why the UAE's exit from Opec is a big deal
stories.summary: It will have little effect on the current oil blockades, but it could change everything afterwards.

stories.title: Lebanon families face second displacement amid ongoing warnings
stories.summary: Families displaced from southern Lebanon set up tents again in Beirut after renewed Israeli warnings.

errors.error: <urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
<urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
